clinical_criteria: Any 3 diagnosis codes on distinct days within the same disease with the first and last diagnoses at least 7 days apart
For T1DM patients: additional exclusion criterion of no type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) codes
Controls must have no autoimmune and auto-inflammatory diagnosis codes
Controls must have no instances of any positive serologies as defined by institutional and assay recommendations
algorithm_summary: The autoimmune disease phenotype algorithm identifies cases and controls across 51 autoimmune diseases spanning 9 disease groups and 6 medical specialties. Cases are defined by having at least one autoimmune disease with 3 diagnosis codes on distinct days within the same disease, with first and last diagnoses at least 7 days apart. The algorithm uses 1,528 diagnosis codes (ICD-9-CM, ICD-10-CM, SNOMED) for case determination. Controls are defined by the absence of any autoimmune diseases, auto-inflammatory conditions, and positive serology tests, using 49,960 diagnosis and measurement codes. Special handling exists for T1DM patients who must not have T2DM codes. The algorithm includes 10 sets of antibody tests for control exclusion, including ANA, ANCA, dsDNA, CCP, RF, and others. Validation involves expert review from multiple specialties including Rheumatology, Nephrology, Dermatology, Neurology, and Gastroenterology.
